summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute code at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp file whose `sources` field (line 6) contains GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)` / `<!@(...)`). npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — even without any declared install/postinstall script — and GYP evaluates command-expansion expressions as a shell/process invocation during the configure step. The result is arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine at `npm install` time. The package presents itself as a Backstage GitLab plugin, a pure-JavaScript/TypeScript frontend/backend integration with no legitimate need for a native addon or for binding.gyp at all; the file's sole effect is to trigger the embedded command during the configure phase. The content of the traced file tripped the malware-output safety filter, consistent with the GYP-command-expansion install-time-RCE fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
